title: DNA Extraction for college laboratory setting

Students need approximately 1.5 hours to extract DNA from up to five samples and the protocol can be carried out over several class periods by stopping at steps 6 and pestle","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a4h5k","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ajl1m","text":"All chemicals were purchased from Fisher Scientific.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Harvest 2-3 seedlings and place in a mortar. Fill with about 50 ml of liquid nitrogen. Grind tissue with pestle.
Add 1 ml of extraction buffer to the tube.
Add 120 µl of 10% SDS. Mix by inverting. If preparing more than one sample, prepare each sample to this step and place on ice.

Add 300 µl 5M KOAc. Mix well by inverting several times ( important !), then place on ice 5 minutes. 00:05:00 Stopping point: Samples can be frozen for a future class period. Thaw samples before starting with step 7.
Pass 700 µl of the supernatant through a miracloth funnel into the second tube.
Add 600 µl of isopropanol. Mix the contents thoroughly by inverting. Stopping point: Samples can be frozen for a future class period.
Carefully pour off and discard the supernatant. Use a P20 set to 20 µl to remove the remaining drops of liquid without disturbing the DNA pellet.
Add 500 µl of 70% ethanol and flick the tube until the pellet comes off the bottom.
Pour off the ethanol. Use a P20 set to 20 µl to remove the remaining drops without disturbing the pellet.
Resuspend the DNA in 50 µl TE and incubate at room temperature for 5 minutes for complete resuspension. Samples should be frozen for storage. 00:05:00
creator: Tracey Depellegrin
